skill_invocation: invokeMarketingSkill("emails", { subjectLine, customerContext, brandVoice, intent: "alternate phrasing", originalBody }, { variants: 2 })
brand_voice: Operator. Jack writes the email, not 'we'. No 'thank you for choosing us' — the deposit already cleared. Plain, concrete, no exclamation points. Signed '— Jack / Day14'.

customerContext.scenario: Sent at 5pm ET by the build agent on a 'good day' (planned-vs-shipped delta within one ticket). Customer reads in the evening, wants the receipt without ceremony.
customerContext.topic: End-of-day customer update on a day where the build is on or ahead of plan. Names what shipped, what's next, links preview + build log.

originalBody:
Today: shipped {{thing_1}} and {{thing_2}}.

Tomorrow: {{thing_3}}.

Preview: {{preview_url}}. Build log: {{buildlog_url}}.

On track for launch {{launch_date}}.

— Jack
Day14

variants.body:
[needs-API-key] Variant 1 of eod-update-good — tighter receipt-style rewrite of:

Today: shipped {{thing_1}} and {{thing_2}}.

[…rest of original preserved verbatim — re-run with marketing-skills:emails wired to populate.]
variants.rationale: placeholder #1 — strategy: tight-receipt (one-line acknowledgement, numbered next steps)

variants.body:

Today: shipped {{thing_1}} and {{thing_2}}.

[…rest of original preserved verbatim — re-run with marketing-skills:emails wired to populate.]
variants.rationale: placeholder #2 — strategy: warmer-ops (single sentence of context before the next step)
